channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A glimpse of Iran, through the eyes of its artists and journalists
item.description: Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.content:encoded:
The Seed of the Sacred Fig, For The Sun After Long Nights, Cutting Through Rocks, It Was Just an Accident, Martyr!, and Kayhan Kalhor.'/>

Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: This quiet epic is the top-grossing Japanese live action film of all time
item.description: The Oscar-nominated Kokuho tells a compelling story about friendship, the weight of history and the torturous road to becoming a star in Japan's Kabuki theater.
item.content:encoded:
The Oscar-nominated Kokuho tells a compelling story about friendship, the weight of history and the torturous road to becoming a star in Japan's Kabuki theater.

(Image credit: Shuichi Yoshida/ASP)
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: For filmmaker Chloé Zhao, creative life was never linear
item.description: Director Chloé Zhao used meditation, somatic exercises and dance to inspire the cast and crew of this Oscar-nominated story about William Shakespeare's family.
item.content:encoded:
Director Chloé Zhao used meditation, somatic exercises and dance to inspire the cast and crew of this Oscar-nominated story about William Shakespeare's family.

(Image credit: Bethany Mollenkof for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: 10 new books in March offer mental vacations
item.description: March is always a big one for books – this year is no different. We call out a handful of upcoming titles for readers to put on their radars — offering a good alternative to doomscrolling.
item.content:encoded: March is always a big one for books – this year is no different. We call out a handful of upcoming titles for readers to put on their radars — offering a good alternative to doomscrolling.
item.dc:creator: Colin Dwyer

item.title: 'Hamnet' star Jessie Buckley looks for the 'shadowy bits' of her characters
item.description: Buckley has been nominated for a best actress Oscar for her portrayal of William Shakespeare's wife in Hamnet. The film "brought me into this next chapter of my life as a mother," Buckley says.
item.content:encoded:
Hamnet.'/>

Buckley has been nominated for a best actress Oscar for her portrayal of William Shakespeare's wife in Hamnet. The film "brought me into this next chapter of my life as a mother," Buckley says.

(Image credit: Kate Green)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Lesley Manville says her stage and screen careers 'feed each other'
item.description: The decorated British actress discusses her new film Midwinter Break, her role in Oedipus on Broadway and how she balances stage and screen.
item.content:encoded:
Midwinter Break.'/>

The decorated British actress discusses her new film Midwinter Break, her role in Oedipus on Broadway and how she balances stage and screen.

(Image credit: Mark de Blok)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Sandwiched
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WXXI listener Jonathan Black and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WXXI listener Jonathan Black and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Found: The 19th century silent film that first captured a robot attack
item.description: A newly rediscovered 1897 short by famed French filmmaker Georges Méliès is being hailed as the first-ever depiction of a robot in cinema.
item.content:encoded:
Gugusse et l'Automate. The pioneering French filmmaker's 1897 short, which likely features the first known depiction of a robot on film, was thought lost until it was found among a box of old reels that had belonged to a family in Michigan and restored by the Library of Congress.'/>

A newly rediscovered 1897 short by famed French filmmaker Georges Méliès is being hailed as the first-ever depiction of a robot in cinema.

(Image credit: The Frisbee Collection)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for February 28. 2026: Live in Bloomington with Lilly King!
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Bloomington, Indiana with host Peter Sagal, special guest Lilly King and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Josh Gondelman, and Faith Salie
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Bloomington, Indiana with host Peter Sagal, special guest Lilly King and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Josh Gondelman, and Faith Salie

(Image credit: François-Xavier Marit)

item.title: 'The Fall and Rise of Reggie Dinkins' falls before it rises — but then it soars
item.description: The unlikely pairing of Tracy Morgan and Daniel Radcliffe isn't quite what makes the show work — it's the exquisite, perfectly-timed jokes that just keep coming.
item.content:encoded:
The unlikely pairing of Tracy Morgan and Daniel Radcliffe isn't quite what makes the show work — it's the exquisite, perfectly-timed jokes that just keep coming.

(Image credit: Scott Gries)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon
